description: PR5 commit 4 — CP-SAT polisher trace emission + leak fix. Minimal fixture: two cross-course sections (CS101, CS102) sharing all 10 students. Slot pool has three options (MON 08:00, MON 09:30, TUE 08:00) and a single lecture room. Test code seeds both placements into the SAME slot (MON 08:00) directly via ORM to simulate a suboptimal state that the V2 multi-strategy greedy would never produce but CP-SAT can repair when called via optimise_current_timetable. CP-SAT's weighted-overlap proxy (shared*5) strongly prefers splitting the pair across two slots; the real evaluator then confirms the improvement. This exercises the commit-4 code path end-to-end: polisher emits CPSAT_IMPROVED trace + improved_sections, V2 overlays them into sections_by_id and decision_trace, and persistence writes the moved placement.
notes: Shared students: 10 (all of ST001-ST010 enrolled in both courses).
weight = 10 * 5 = 50 per co-located meeting-pair => CP-SAT strongly prefers to split.
Test seeds both placements at MON 08:00 via ORM then calls optimise_current_timetable with run_local_search=False/run_chain_search=False so CP-SAT runs on the seeded state directly.
Real evaluator confirms lexicographic_score strictly improves; polisher returns improved_sections + decision_trace; V2 persists.
